title: Coelogyne purpurascens
taxonomy: Coelogyne purpurascens is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Coelogyne, which comprises over 200 species of terrestrial and epiphytic orchids. The genus Coelogyne is part of the subfamily Epidendroideae, which is one of the largest and most diverse subfamilies of orchids. Coelogyne purpurascens can be found in tropical regions of Asia, where it typically grows in humid forests and mountainous areas. The classification of Coelogyne purpurascens reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations to its native habitat.

intro: Coelogyne purpurascens, also known as the Purple Coelogyne, is a type of orchid native to tropical Asia, characterized by its vibrant purple flowers and robust stem. Research has shown that the plant possesses various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne purpurascens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Coelogyne purpurascens is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are valued for their potential to boost immune function and alleviate respiratory ailments.
